title: Astondoa 40 “Fufi”
subtitle: Spanish-built 12.5 m motor yacht — at home in Puerto Banús. Skipper, fuel, drinks and snacks included on every charter.
author: Boat Rental In Marbella · BoatHire24

sections.heading: Overview
sections.html:
The Astondoa 40 — known as “Fufi” — is the Spanish equivalent of the classic Mediterranean motor yacht. Built in Cádiz by Astondoa, fitted out in teak and cream leather, and at home anchoring off the Golden Mile for a long lunch.

At 12.5 metres with room for nine guests, she sits in the sweet spot for families and small groups: big enough for shade, a saloon and two private cabins, small enough to feel like your own boat. Every charter sails with a licensed skipper, and fuel is included in the price.
sections.image_caption: Guests relaxing on the bow of the Astondoa 40 at Puerto Banús marina

sections.heading: On-board highlights
sections.html:
• Flybridge with shaded bimini and second helm
  • Cream-leather saloon with table-for-six and AC
  • Two private cabins for overnight charter
  • Spanish-built (Astondoa, Cádiz) — local provenance
  • Snorkel gear, inflatable donut & paddleboard on board
sections.image_caption: Cream-leather saloon with table-for-six and teak finish

sections.heading: Pricing & inclusions
sections.html:
The Astondoa 40 charters by the hour from Puerto Banús, with a 2-hour minimum. All prices are total — not per person — and include VAT.

DurationPrice (EUR)
2 hours€749
3 hours€1,049
4 hours€1,299
5 hours€1,549
6 hours€1,799
7 hours€2,099
8 hours€2,299

Every charter includes:

  • Licensed skipper
  • Fuel
  • Drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava)
  • Light snacks
  • Insurance
  • Safety equipment
  • VAT (21%)

sections.heading: Departure & booking info
sections.html:
The Astondoa 40 departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s flagship marina. Exact berth details are sent with your booking confirmation — plan to arrive about 15 minutes before departure so the skipper can run the welcome briefing and get you under way on time.

Popular routes include a swim-stop anchorage off the Golden Mile, a coastal cruise towards Cabopino, or a sunset loop back into Puerto Banús. Your skipper will tailor the route to the day’s conditions and your group’s plans.
sections.image_caption: Arriving back at Puerto Banús marina at sunset
